name: Delete the middle node in LL
category: Learn LinkedList [Single LL, Double LL, Medium, Hard Problems]
statement:
Given the head of a non-empty singly linked list containing integers, delete the middle node of the linked list. Return the head of the modified linked list.


The middle node of a linked list of size n is the (⌊n / 2⌋ + 1)th node from the start using 1-based indexing, where ⌊x⌋ denotes the largest integer less than or equal to x.
constraints:
• 1 <= number of nodes in the Linked List <= 105
  • 0 <= ListNode.val <= 104

examples.explanation: n = 5. ⌊n / 2⌋ + 1 = 3, therefore middle node has index 3 and so the node with value 3 was deleted.

examples.output: head -> 7 -> 6 -> 4 Expl﻿anation: n = 4. ⌊n / 2⌋ + 1 = 3, therefore middle node has index 3 and so the node with value 5 was deleted.

testcases.name: Case 2
testcases.expected: head -> 7 -> 6 -> 4 Expl﻿anation: n = 4. ⌊n / 2⌋ + 1 = 3, therefore middle node has index 3 and so the node with value 5 was deleted.
